v1 Alcohol -> v2 GHB/Phenibut

The future is open-sourced, and decentralized.

Capitalism got us to build these miraculous things: Spotify gave us universal access to music, and YouTube gave us billions of videos! In a way, thank God for capitalism and the promise of huge profits off of users.

Ofc, they came at a cost; if you don't pay these companies for premium subscriptions, they slap annoying, invasive, personalized ads all over your content. 

The evil part is that these companies' goals are to make money, and NOT to increase consumers' access to content.

They are motivated to worsen user experience: YT doesn't let you natively download videos, YT's aggressive copyright system takes down tons of people's vids (& $$$) w no way to fight back.

Spotify blocks some artists.

The essence of the unspoken evil: The collective intelligence comes to rely on YT for ALL VIDEOS just like a person relies on their memory, but YT feels no obligation to acknowledge that that is their role in society.

This means YT/Spotify can put barrier after barrier preventing access to their content, bc they know they can extort users who have come to rely on it as an extension of their own mind. This is like an organ holding the body hostage!!

But necessity breeds innovation. The beautiful future is one of OPEN SOURCE CODE, where communities get together to collectively build/iterate on a platform to share music. The work is distributed and the focus is 100% on giving the user the best possible experience.

Open-source, decentralized YT & Spotify will come from a very different heart than the platforms we see today, offering unprecedented levels of customization, integration, and extension. By not being greedy, they truly represent the user, and become the best possible platform.

Some ideas: -native YT downloads
-native YT video editor like Adobe Premiere
-non-predatory race-to-the-bottom dopamine-exploiting reccomendation algorithms
-spotify not shoving ads down your throat; you're in control of suggestions
-spotify add-ons like music visualizers

-spotify support for lossless music files and [decentralized] music downloading in general (thinking abt my music torrent sites here)
-additional features like artist-neutral shuffle 
-perhaps Spotify integration with it's own simple DAW like soundtrap so u can make music too

The point is, collaboration and decentralization represent true digital democracy. Rn companies take advantage of us bc they can and it will literally only get worse until users create their own platforms, which they can, and de-throne digital puppeteers pulling our brain strings

Music 4 all, truly! Internet renaissance! Videos 4 all, with active effort to reduce barriers and authentic intentions! Education/music/content/culture Renaissance!
User-first experience, user-first platform creation, user-first everything!
LET US SEE YOUR CODE!!! 👨‍💻🤓
Alcohol is dumb, build a better drug

(1/x) S4E1 Rick and Morty had a great premise that actually totally validated an idea I'd had since I was a kid; the "causality-hack". In S4E1, Morty comes across a "death crystal" which allows him to see his future death extrapolated from his current circumstances. https://t.co/ihyDd0powy

(2/x) Morty sees his death with Jessica, and then it briefly fades away. He stumbles around until he sees that death again, and eventually becomes this machine-learning algorithm that optimizes for "death with jessica!!", doing extremely strange, specific things to get there. https://t.co/zj0jTjdKG7

(3/x) I've had this feeling that there probably exists some quantumly perfect combination of words/actions that, were you supernaturally given their knowledge, would confer the ultimate level of agency, or a literal/metaphorical "get out of jail free" card.

(4/x) Morty literally uses his causality-hack to get out of jail. His final words to the judge after being sentenced were "I will always remember our time in Peru"--the last words of the judge's deceased husband. She acquitted Morty!

(5/x) This could be extrapolated to the physical world too; squeaking a chair against the ground in a *particular way* perhaps causes quantum fluctuations in a butterfly-effect manner causing something miraculous, like extinction of all coronaviruses. its Knowledge = True Power https://t.co/DjwnemdN3T

(6/x) The idea is similar to "adversarial examples" in machine learning; tweak something in an adversarial way to game the system of probabilities in order to get the outcome you really want. I've no idea how we can accomplish this in real life. But my is it a fun idea! https://t.co/lFlIT48Iyv

This just in: metta is incredible. But I think it's even more versatile than people realize. Metta + IFS is like Metta².

Metta already suggests a kind of tree-like structure, with the parent node of 'all beings' and the child nodes of each individual being. 💛

You can throw metta at any one of them (or any grouping), or all of them at once.

(Right now I'm loving https://t.co/KDCHLMA62n:
"So to produce equanimity towards yourself, just notice how you're always doing the best you can. You're just you.") 💛

But when you combine Metta with IFS, you get: The Fractal Metta Tree.

You can do metta on your parts! You can even do metta *from the perspective* of your parts! Now there's a whole bunch more metta you can do! So many new perspectives and combinations!💛

(Hell, do metta from your parts on other people's parts! Get your parts into agreement on all the levels!) The dissonance is there, if only you adopt the introspective tools capable of holding it in perspective & ironing it out.💛

Don't act as if the world is going to end. 
Even if "your" world does end in climate apocalypse, present you is sowing the seeds for ALL possible future worlds that might branch off from this one.
Don't fail to help others for the sake of a bad future that is certainly not given.

in our case of imminent climate catastrophe, there are PLENTY of worlds where things turn out better. Don't give up on your life thinking that it's all for nothing--the yous that inherit a world that has averted disaster will realize it was you who chose catastrophe in the end.

Edit: the Metta Tree goes even deeper: doing metta on (or from) each of the individual sensations (sub-sub-agents) within an IFS sub-agent consciousness.

After finding metamodernism, I feel extremely seen--I've found my vague intuitions of a synthesis-oriented meta-paradigm are actually part of larger, valid, cultural paradigm.

Meta-modernism is an inclusive synthesis of modernism & post-modernism:

https://t.co/3cq5ZaIsPI

Modernism: we must try new things and question assumptions to make progress in finding the best possible way of living. Appeared partly due to industrial revolution.

Keywords: progress, innovation, reason, universality
Examples: Utopianism, abstract art, artistic criticism

Postmodernism: there is no single best possible way of living, only people who think their way is best. Therefore any idea of “progress” is not linear or universal, but the product of an agenda. Language and power influence each other. Appeared partly due to the rise of fascism..

Keywords: deconstruction, pluralism, critique of power

Examples: Dystopian fiction, remixes, cut-ups

Metamodernism: modernist optimism and postmodernist critique are two opposing poles we must oscillate between. We must create things to be critiqued, and we must critique things as a creative act. ...

By simultaneously constructing and deconstructing, we can have creativity without dogma, and progress without agenda.

Keywords: oscillation, synthesis

Examples: Magical realism, hyperpop

Check out my Metamodernism playlist to see my ever-evolving interpretation of metamodernism, the ways I'm touched by it, and the ways it's important to me, expressed in the medium of forward-thinking/future-oriented music.

https://t.co/RHg6KbgdfE

my build-a-better-weed posts hinge on the presumption that there *is* a better weed worth building out there. i'd like to formalize this idea if i can, so we can all come together on a conclusion of whether or not this idea is feasible.

I used to worry about optimizing consumption mechanism, like bong instead of pipe, then dry herb vape (dat flavor + precise temp control doe), then finally edibles. For 1, yes it's great not burning up all your entourage-fx terpenes and eliminating intake of cancerous substances.

I imagined the next step being laser rigs vaporizing weed perfectly.
But on the other hand, these alterations dont make weed perfect. Yes, vape v bong is quite different. (Try a dab, sir!) 
But ever since my first hit, I've been looking for capital-W Weed... and haven't found it.

I've imagined engineering the perfect strain to treat the stress of PTSD/cPTSD without paranoia, anxiety, munchies, memory impairment, reduced executive function. CRISPR'd Weed. Perfect Weed. My Weed.
Everything you love about weed, better, with none of the "side-effects".

In a recent conversation I realized, you can bypass finnicky biological cannabis organisms all together--you can Super Shulgin weed's ass. Turns out you can build your own weed carts, mixing PG/whatever liquid with various terpenes and cannabinoids for your own species of Weed.

Now that we have our M-rated version of Build-a-Bear, the question remains: can we build a better Weed? Are strains *really* that different from one another that it warrants specific concoctions for particular ends? The science has been sluggish on these phenomenological matters.

After all, arent the munchies a universal weed experience? Paranoia? Scatter-brained-ness?
Yes and no! 
https://t.co/3PMKOPBICM is the world's best database for phenom. reports b/n strains, and it suggests lil micro-universes of variation in b/n these various strains.

The battles of sativa vs indica vs hybrid have been long fought, and will be long-fought after we're gone. "Try a different strain bro" is a common annoying refrain. And yet, we can't get away from the fact that diff strains have different utilities. AM sativas, PM indicas.

I take this one to wake up, I take this one when I go to the gym, I take this one for socializing, I take this one when I want to trip out, I take this one to eat a lot of food, I take this one to sleep.
Weed simply *does* present like this, and users use it this way, too.

We need to hear what people are sayin; we need to design utility-based Weed, make it less messy if we can, and more directional. How about weed that *specifically* makes you hate your mom less during din-din? (+ makes you hungry while still being clear-headed for conversation)

We need weed that makes you more creative so you can start your rap career, without making you so sluggish you need ghost writers. Weed that gives you the energy/motivation to work out, without the munchies that reset your progress. And fuck the paranoia, what's up with that?

Have you personally experienced wide variation in qualia between different strains of weed? Have some explicitly provided you more positive experiences/bliss than others? Can you imagine a world where these differences are exponentially more pronounced?

A world where 'Weed' means something so vast and complex that it implies its own world worth exploring, a microcosm in the state space of consciousness mirroring the differences between LSD and Ketamine?

Personally i have strong suspicions weed is hiding something from us (lol)--that it's much better of a playground for consciousness than it's letting on. If you can also see such a world, do comment or DM me. I love thinking & talking about this stuff. Thus,

Let there be Weed!

~Cody's Music Qualia Reviews~
Thread #1:
Pinkshinyultrablast's 'Everything Else Matters'
Part 1: Why Is This Special To Me?
This is the kind of music that makes me lean back in awe of its grandiosity. It's so big and massive and rich in an ethereally-textured way. :) https://t.co/MQwJHpqgZD

To me, this music is alive, and it speaks to life. It provides a richness + expansiveness that I can often only get with drugs. To me it's Transhumanist Music, and I don't plan to stop recommending it anytime soon. This is my attempt to explain why I believe it so valuable.
I'm also tweeting this bc I couldn't figure out how to save a draft on Twitter for PC, so I have to finish what I started: it's now or never. Here goes:

Part 2: Spatial Depth: wow so big!
Reverb -> delay -> reverb -> delay to more reverb, back to delay... when does it end? It doesnt!
Music sounds great--full, luscious, rich--when we slap on a bunch of reverb, esp when we do it in the right ways. This music is called "shoegaze"

for a reason. Like My Bloody Valentine, they construct a big ol' wall of sound that has so much going on that you seem to lose yourself in it. This makes it a practical escape from everyday woes that seem to physically shrink in comparison.

Part 3: Exploit: Infinite* Emotional Depth available through Paradox
Something about the reverb'd feminine falsettos, the ones that come in soft-spoken and sacredly serious, yet can't be heard under all the layers of reverb and fade out likely never deciphered for their content.

Form w/out function, a purposeless word said to be lost, known only by god. Sometimes you can make out a song name, maybe "holy forest". It's a marvelous contradiction that makes me clap like a giddy child and pay serious attention like I'm at church. Now that's a cool church!

Sometimes this tug and pull between the sacred hymnic/melodic seriousness/intentionality and the 99.9% undecipherability create an anxiety that makes you want to at once laugh and cry and scream and pray and keep dancing ring around the rosie, until they all fall down--then get

up and do it all again. It's intense and exhausting and so REAL, there's blood wiped across this album--yet it at the same time feels entirely sterile and detached, zookeeper-god-looking-through-the-plexiglass vibes.
We are reminded, for all the insane depth of our experience, aren't we all far-away zookeeper gods in our own untouchable worlds? Ketamine'd observers?

And for all the detached-ness, aren't we all inextricably IN THIS? In this life, alive, breathing, and able to be touched by something like a music? We are worth observing, too, to the very end as experience fades away into emptiness.

Part 4: Temporal Scrambling, Noise vs Beauty
This kind of music asks us about it, "how do I even exist?" Touche. Feeling so complicated, some kind of an accidental system so complex that it's living and breathing and looking in the mirror and asking if a god created it, and if so

whether it can go back. There's a war between signal and noise going on, a series of battles expressively undulating throughout the album. Sometimes it's the layers of differently-timed delay + reverbs that leave you wondering as a listener "where the hell even am I right now?"

Am I at ten seconds ago, 30 seconds ago? then: does it even matter? I'm here, dammit. You give up the fight to discern, to HAVE to know; you surrender to the experience like an LSD trip, and now that all the bullshit ego narratives are over, you Just Feel Good(TM).

At some points you lose your posturing that the textures forming on top aren't the main feature--that they aren't somehow WAY more important, real, recognizable, meaningful than the very content of the music that causally *created* them all the way down the signal chain.

This is nowhere more evident than the very end of the album, where the long battle b/n S/N is FINALLY won by noise, who takes over the album until it becomes so cavernously all-encompassing for so long you forget you're listening to it (really): you forget to do listening.

And then there's just music, hanging there, ~phenomenally unexperienced~, until its expansion has expanded so far that there becomes nowhere & nothing left to expand into, and the noise appropriately dies, having served its purpose, and becomes silence.

And, somehow, the silence is even louder: you experience silence. It's visceral. It's even agressive. The album's over. But it's not. The silence is more of a music than the music, more of a form than an emptiness, more of a listening than what you were just "listening" to...

ADHD 🧵 where I explore the nature of my own experience with executive dysfunction

ADHD being a learned emotional stance towards The Future that says "I can't just finish my current task/thought/intention, else I will be caught unprepared later, when it matters."

Today I received a letter that I wrote to myself a year ago today (1/12/21). I want to share the beginning part, which resembles a poem. It's about social connection, & being in the world. I don't remember writing the letter, I do remember that I wrote it:

Meeting new people for the first time is always the hardest. A slow coming into the room, a gradual presence in which I don’t want to be, the present, to which I would rather say “no”. Each moment is like this, in which ‘I’ am born anew.

Saying “hello” is the hardest. Each time you meet me, I am a different me. Each time I meet you, I’m afraid you’ll see. I’m afraid you’ll expect the same person, and you’ll see someone much, much worse.
I say hello to you every day, and each time it breaks my heart. I don’t make eye contact, but I can’t look away.
My body is made of me, and everywhere I look ‘I’ am nowhere to be found. When I do find me, it’s implied in the curdled pain that’s knotted and real.
This is the real me that you see, that is forced against the stream to go and meet you very briefly. I lose myself thousands of times before I look into your eyes.

...
The letter gets into some deep shit, some risk/reward about choosing to be alive. 
and it ends with:

"Bless up, bliss up."

Yesterday while driving I decided to fuck around and feel my feet in my shoes, and then feel my shoes on the floor, and I ended up feeling the bottom of the car and its wheels gripping the road. Had no idea 1) how ungrounded I am, 2) how awesome being grounded feels

I was trying to ground myself because my awareness was collapsed and i was driving skittishly. In the moments where I felt the car, I felt drastically more at ease driving. I wonder if most people--ostensibly being more grounded--feel more like this when driving?
Low key was trying to do Alexander Technique despite not knowing truly what it is nor how to do it. I wanted to expand my awareness to improve my driving, and at least that aspect of it worked.

This is kinda what I take "magic" to be. I think magic in this sense (working definition: trying to do things you're not sure are possible) could help to bootstrap myself out of sub-optimal mental & physical states.

Therefore, let this poste be an invitation for ur magic tips™️
What strategies do you use to expand your awareness or otherwise make funny things happen that you probably couldnt make happen through the sheer will of your skeptical epistemological rationality?

Why is it that when we try to do Education--and whip children into a shape where they will put out millions of effort units--we come away with something so un-educational, so far removed from its original intentions?

This is why #firstprinciples thinking is so important to me (forgive the hashtag, but I find it convenient to organize thoughts). Keeping your pragmatic intentions in mind at all points in the building journey so you dont end up divorcing your human roots for something inhumane.
The alternative is a cosmically absurd adherence to traditionalism in spite of the most obvious of evidence. AP history has a purpose, but at what cost??

It comes to a point where you see ancient shitposting, hilarious, alive, and immensely inherently valuable, and know that it will not get its day in court when it comes to being recognized and disseminated as "history worth learning".

To be fair, and like with everything I see wrong w the world, it's IMPOSSIBLE not to make some mistakes, to miss some teaching opportunities and accidentally commit abuse. But being perfect is not the standard, it's the aspiration. We see novel ways we can improve & we update.

(104/104) And so thats why we should abolish school and send our children out into the woods with no contact or supplies for 10 weeks as a coming of age rite of passage and give all our jobs and love to the remaining survivors.

@nowtheo -Psychedelic amusement park, one park for each class. Stimulants would be a high speed rush. 
Depressants are somehow slow euphoria not sure how. Blurry, diffuse warm glowy. Blackness for blackouts. Access to an experience of conscious ness that is not otherwise available.

@nowtheo Somewhere have a massive quantity of things/fractals. Amorphous objects that transition into other objects. Salvia land has cone trees and eyes that watch you, whispering in your ear, shaken and spooked. Strobe lights for phasing/frame effect. Uncanny valley everything.

@nowtheo Maybe "mirrors" show no one on the ride, lr other people on the ride. The music skips, repeats, stops. The rollercoaster, like my wearable back massager, touches you. You are someone else. (A link in a chain?) Somehow invoke homonculus.

@nowtheo Truman Show, cameras everywhere, everything is pretending to be real, a simulation. The city pretends to continue in certain areas, a wall with painted sky. Your ride stops and every person acts normal until they stop and stare at you in silence (how is this scalable?).

@nowtheo You have to crawl through a small passage on your hands and knees. Rides where you become part of the ride. The idea of mickey mouse is salvia esque itself, mickey mouse snowflakes are fractaly. One sound "A" the whole ride that slowly evolves in dissonant intensity,
@nowtheo singular voice to multiple voices until yelling. Visuals are flashed to you or the letter A in static structures around the ride. "You" being mirrored in interesting ways. Augmented reality?

@nowtheo [END NOTES] so, these are some interesting ideas and it seems i got a lot less written down than i thought. it seems most of that was about "salvia land" lmao. i wrote this at disney land, thinking about what was missing and how i would design a park.

I'm back! I come bearing thoughts from the outside realms (1/6)

I really, really enjoyed my twitter break. i want to do more of that. 'what did you learn, o wise cody?' you ask?
1. I figured out how I want to make art in the world, starting with abstract processes of just collecting things that I like--be they colors, words, phrases, shapes,

...data structures. I'll amass these atoms of creativity, use them as building blocks to manifest my design.
2. I liked having my thoughts feed back into the strange loop. i dont have to worry about exporting perfectly or sexy

3. i liked streaming a lot and even got a few active viewers. THIS IS SO FUN.
4. i like the extended focus to dive into work & life. I liked the silence, no longer distracting myself on purpose and jamming my mind with a flurry of random thoughts from others. __space to think__.

Seriously, streaming is going to feature prominently in this next season of 'cody writes stuff'. I hope many of you come join me on twitch and make fun of me or (preferably) make fun of the world with me. some of my favorite things to do include ableton & python.

Morning pages are one of the best things to ever happen to me. 1-3 pages a day, write out all my bullshit, no inner critic everything is okay, iterate on being the best channel of that improvisational force & get out all the junk thoughts in the way of your real writing. 🧵✨
And in the process, find your voice. (Yeah, that part of yourself that you don't hate everything that comes out)

As a result of this practice I've written far more *good shit* than I've probably written like ever. All thrown up like accidents. My previous mode of writing was hyperselfjudgemental in form, its whole stressful purpose to please teachers and get me good marks.
For the first time ever I am regularly able to get into a creative flow while writing.

But no time for relaxing and celebrating just yet. I needed to share this because I finally started writing my own personal philosophies. My values, my valuable beliefs! But there is much much work to be done to widen this channel. Consistent practice must continue. ✍️

"wikipedia is not a valid source"
Did you just cripple a generation's ability to rapidly learn & follow their passions using one of the most advanced informational technologies to date?

Wikipedia is such a blessing for being a transparent, free, endlessly hyperlinked, & open source compendium of knowledge. Contributing to Wikipedia is doing an unambiguously good thing (though I'd prefer to see it integrate with wikiHow for more practical instrumental help).

I can seriously see an argument for government funding ppl who just want to contribute to Wikipedia all day. Here's $30,000, go learn about rice and ants and hearing and doors for a year. And leopard spots and 4d cubes and linoleum tiles and Gilgamesh and etymology and follicles.

That person just seeded like 4 people's future life passion over the next 3 decades. "Yeah I just saw this article on the pattern of leopard spots and I realized it's all of ever really cared about, it described my life's struggles perfectly. Thank god that info was out there."

Wish I could tell all my trauma peeps that
"I had a bad experience with Thing" =\= "Thing is Bad". 

This is one of those confusions that requires multiple dependencies to unravel, including being able to simultaneously validate and challenge yourself.

In many ways I'm still realizing this myself and each time you realize it it's quite painful. Registering the extent of your skewed perception is painful, like who's going to make it okay for me to have seen things so wrong? It's up to me to comfort and accept myself.

Dont want to share any concrete examples, but a lot of it is interactions with people, especially unsavory/unsafe interactions with parental figures at an impressionable age. These perceptions need updating and expanding--people are complex, not black and white.

I say "wish I could tell my trauma peeps" because you can't share this kind of perspective with them--it will invalidate their experience of having a Bad Thing happen to them. And in bad cases they haven't even been safe enough/able to validate that bad childhood experience yet.

To the person recovering from a bad experience, challenging their perspective looks to them like a return to the past where they believed it "wasn't really that bad", a minimization of their experience. Which they *know* to not be the case, their powerful child emotions say so

However, one slowly learns by themselves that their childhood perspective, while valid, doesn't say all there is to know about the situation. Painful to acknowledge, but there's more to the person that did the Bad Thing. There's things you like about them and kind things they did

Having room for this nuance, normally not tolerated by either perspective (they're either good OR bad), is a sign of growth. It's weird but you need to cherish what you enjoyed so you can get more of it, while vehemently protesting bad stuff. This is how you escape distortion

Just discovered that there is a scale for social anxiety. Cool! https://t.co/7dPi53M4f1
Cooler: it's called your 'SUDS level'.
The thing is, I love socializing and find myself an extremely social creature. I just love talking to people and soaking in their energy and throwing it back into the world.
Despite this, I've spent half of my life coasting at pretty high levels (maybe like 50, and at my worst I get scattered to an 80.)

I'm learning that I wasn't born that way, that it has to do with amygdala hyper-activation. That my nervous system has a small "window of tolerance", easily getting into states of hyper- and hypo-arousal. And that regulation can be learned. But I have to learn it

(I went from never even noticing the distress -> believing social distress was normal -> realizing it and believing Im stuck this way forever -> increasingly aware that my nervous system swings between too 'on' and shut off.)

So I'm excited to learn how to regulate my nervous system, and expand my window of tolerance. Seeing this scale I feel validated and less alone, knowing that there's other people like me and there are paths out of the suffering that sometimes seems like it's all there is.

For anyone curious and daring enough, this complicated post (I skim it every time I open it, but I do open it) is what inspired me to tweet on this topic of nervous system regulation:
https://t.co/Epp1xwBHwQ

-i took the very low dose (1.5mg) b4 bed

-vivid dreams are NO JOKE. started with a more detailed imagination in the hypnagogic states before totally drifting off. surprising detail
-would probably recommend this to people for this effect alone (many of my friends sadly report they lack any dream recall)

-higher excitability, but not noticeably more anxious.
-OCD was increased noticeably. despite this is fell asleep fine
-the first 1-3 days are supposed to be when any slightly adverse symptoms occur, so this is expected. luckily it's nbd

-sleep quality: heartbeat seemed faster, but my oura ring says my heart rate overnight reached the lowest it's been in the last 2 weeks (finally!).
-& My average HRV immediately also achieved a maximum (good!) for the last 2 weeks.

-i still woke up multiple times throughout the night as is normal, but each time as if from an *incredibly restful sleep*. it was absurd, the feeling was distinct and strong, as if i took some ultra-powerful form of magnesium glycinate. so comfy
-upon waking, feel tired as normal

overall:
-wow! i expected to take a week to feel good, and thought id have to feel worse before i felt better from receptor upregulation--but this goofy positivity was immediate.
-downsides minimal but slightly distracting. should disappear in a few days.
-totes a net benefit! 👍

The one thing that has me anxious about beautiful things like tpot is Charles sobrahj-types lurking in the background, taking advantage of vulnerabilities in open-minded/pro-vulnerability group dynamics. 
The q I've been asking is: how to memetically vaccinate against bad actors?
Might fuck around and ignite the flame of life, rising like a phoenix from the ashes of past versions of myself that no longer serve me.

Some bad habits of mine were able to drop like flies and I'm really grateful for that, good riddance. 

Others are more persistent and frustrating. May the life force energy find me letting go of these too as it travels through me and out of me

Beyond the frame of good and bad, I'm referring to the category of people who "want to cause harm intentionally or instrumentally". This is kind of my version of X-risk for the community trauma healing space.

Community trauma healing/interdependence is beautiful and special, fuelled by the willingness to get vulnerable. But the same vulnerability is virgin fields for someone skilled at spotting and gaming those vulnerabilities for their own gain.

This one pattern comes up over and over when helping people through trauma responses: validate, challenge, validate, challenge, validate, challenge.

It's amazing. Your problems are 100% real but there's something...

There's always something about your response/reaction/interpretation to the circumstance that can basically change it such that it's not a problem anymore. Same circumstances that caused you distress, only now the perspective is different and almost no distress is caused.
In this way you can, without necessarily widening your "window of tolerance" (aka ability to endure stress and stay chilling), adapt to and grow from new and difficult circumstances.

Usually the impulse is to scrunch up and get all >:( in response to a problem. "THIS SUCKS!!". Gotta "yes, and" that b*tch: how are we selling ourselves short, closing off from the full truth, not feeling gratitude, denying ourself agency for the sake of relishing being a victim?

One key trick is to be aware of when your inner critic pipes up with a criticism, or you cringe at yourself. Then, don't even think about it, but reinforce just the *opposite* of the criticism you levied against yourself.
This builds a habit of healthy & compassionate self relating, leading to a self-model that is more trustworthy because it is not based in fear and avoidance behavior.

I've been doing pullups lately, more each day! It feels great to get better at something you love!

The feeling of gripping the bar, and pulling the weight of your body up with the strength of your back, the muscles contracting in synchrony, knowing just what to do--to actually attempt such a feat, let alone succeed at it again and again!

You don't have to tell each individual muscle what to do--you can't, even if you tried to, there's just too many of them. They work as a system, and you trust them with your life that they can take care of the physical realm. All you have to do is: try to do a pull-up.

It also feels good to give alternative medicine a chance without being sneery and needing it to fit into my framework about how the world works. Suspending disbelief for a second can make your experience of the world that much more enjoyable.

I've stepped into an awareness that is a little wider, enough to notice the patterns my personality follows and its inherent virtues and limitations!

Like Self 1 (the 'I') being aware of Self 2 (the doer) & having gratitude for it, and seeing how it is unique just being itself

Like I feel I've done a good job at being Cody over the course of these two decades! Not in a historical sense but in a, all of my automatic behaviors and patterns in the present are built out of intentions that I can understand and respect, sense. Go me!

I get why it's so hard for ppl to understand me, and have greater empathy for my social struggles now, feeling a bit less judgy of myself (Self 2) for just being me. 

I bring a lot to the table, with such dedicated emphasis to good taste, open-mindedness, and being original.

before: whaa..? what the fuck do i eat?
after: i'm low on protein right now, let me cook some eggs with spinach, tomatoes, and sausage for lunch (L:

the words that came up on my meditation retreat were: "i want to find out the core pillars of healthy eating", although my main problem was "how do i know what foods to eat and when? how do i pair them together tastily? [when do i stop eating?]"

fasting for a couple days during the retreat booted me into a really high-grade flow of imagining tasty food combinations i wanted to try (some of them inspirationally healthy, some of them ambitiously gluttonous). at one point a new food gestalt would arrive at me every 5sec

that passion + "i allow myself to buy expensive health foods" + calorie-tracking has made it really easy to create and plan meals. 

additionally, my body operates best on two meals/day, so my passion for cooking doesn't get tired up by the logistics of making food thrice/day.

have you personally found a balance between the two? are they inherently at odds, or is it possible to rein in your creativity so that you can access it at a regular cadence?

my thoughts go to creatives like visakan who chase the midnight coffee, and hunter s thompson, who had less than sustainable methods for acessing creativity.

my theory is that each excursion out of the schedule seems temporarily helpful but in the long run anti-helpful

further: theory is that creativity can be stabilized and it's about opening/widening the channel, not about forcing it open through the manic rhythm of drugs or anti-sleep binges. the creative path & exp is not widely understood, and having project timelines makes this worse

curation is a form of art; it can completely change the way i engage with content vs content that is just sitting out there.

I just got an MRI for the first time and I REALLY enjoyed it.

The tech asked "have you ever been to a rave before? It's like a boring rave."
---
It was NOT boring! It was *enthralling*!! So many challenging industrial sounds.

It was like the person who wrote it *did not care* what you'd think of it. Which is technically true. The sounds were purely functional--but their textures and slow evolution or reversion was so curious. I was always listening to see what I would hear next. It was not boring.

And the bass was so effectively transduced into my body, I was thinking "man screw subpacs, we should all have MRI machines for our next rave".

It was legit a full-body experimental challenging techno-industrial rave experience.

It didn't even have any flashing or strobing lights, just complete darkness, and I liked that. The whole experience was SO anti-kitsch (optimizing exactly zero for the listener and yet being so gratifying) that i think I was to hear more of this. I wonder what else I need MRI'd..
Y'all I highly recommend getting an MRI

I don't mean to ignore suffering or shut out reality. 

I am talking about the possibility for reframing, for optimism and attraction, for seeing possibilities where you thought there weren't. There are mental tools that can jailbreak you

I'm taking about the feeling where you see the same X (eg your room) and it appears/feels *way different* because you just came back from vacation/a long day at work/just stayed in all day.

⭐Use/abide in that space between stimulus and response to mold your reality for bliss.

A big hole opens up in the middle of NYC times square, people start jumping in and there's no explanation. The phenomenon ramps up, until there's 50 people a day jumping in this gaping 10ft radius hole. It appears to go down thousands of feet. They can't be stopped by logic

News stations gather and don't leave, self-appointed dissuaders standing vigilantly but ineffectively for long hours, construction workers throwing their hands up and scratching their heads and smoking big cigars and kicking their feet up while puffing and watching it all

Psychologists at first worry that it's infectious, like some kind of thought-meme that will spread by its mere exposure, people getting stuck on the idea that they have to jump in. But it doesnt spread, only a steady stream of 50 people/day, with meaningful lives, jumping

Many are locals, some buying plane tickets just for the act. Dissuaders asking them "why? Why are you doing this?" No sadness or upset in their faces, nor peace or grimness. Nor mania/grandiosity. Just consignment to fate + mild directed determination. Few word responses, vague

After 5 days of this, it is discovered that ppl jumping in the hole are reappearing in countries around the world, found wearing different clothes, speaking diff languages, telling complex and intricate stories about who they are. Somehow, the jumpers are reappearing as diff ppl

It made little sense but it's all anyone can talk about for awhile. Could you jump into a hole that changes your life? Could everybody get a total life reset if they so desired? Everybody talked about whether they would jump in the hole, whether they should. Confused, hopeful ppl

honestly i aspire for us to have unconvictable presidents. by that i mean, people as awesome as @slatestarcodex, whom i generally trust to do what's right in the world because I have a general sense of their sincerity levels.

@slatestarcodex I think our prev presidents have been super convictable (looking at you, Andrew Johnson et al). the only barrier being, no one wants to be the first one to convict a president. the bandwagon against trump seems to have made it easier to get over that hump.

@slatestarcodex No one is above the law, by "unconvictable" I moreso meant "you would never really need to think about convicting these people, they're my friend[s] and hopefully can be the nation's friend[s] too". I want more presidents like that, and I think we can have them, too.

@slatestarcodex We need to overhaul the stable but worn systems we use to elect our nation's leaders. The electoral college is an obvious target, tho I cant say what I would replace it with. Maybe it starts with overhauling the systems that would make the educated decision about a new voting sys

@slatestarcodex Liquid democracy (as a hybrid between representative and direct democracy) piques my attn as a way to make populance-endorsed decisions. Again, I would probably choose Scott Alexander to be my 'representative' on a lot of issues (as well as half of tpot).

@slatestarcodex Another benefit of liquid democracy is that it's referential. I choose which issues I trust Scott to vote for me on, and for each issue he can also outsource his view to his chosen experts, who may themselves pass on some of their votes to a chosen expert.

@slatestarcodex This system is far from secure and needs a lot of work. But it's a thought that could get us to a place of stable decision-making, where all citizens can feel good about having a voice, from the hyperlocal to the federal.

@slatestarcodex We could elect officials we trust to make the right, extremely well-thought-out, decisions about replacing the electoral college with some other system that produces a more ideal representation of the beliefs of the people.

@slatestarcodex Democracy is an unsupported Operating System with lingering day 0 exploits that weren't relevant until many years from 1776. Direct democracy and indirect democracy each have their flaws, overall would love to see an overhaul in the world's/nation's/city's fractal decisionmaking

there is a certain type of self-actualization, here in the internet age, that requires having an extremely well-curated content algorithm

on most social media sites, we curate our feeds whether we mean to or not. on twitter, we choose who we follow, optimizing for what resonates. on reddit, we subscribe to subreddits. on tiktok, it happens automatically, designing a hyperaddictive feed that was never meant 2 exist.

on YT, I am lucky that my algorithm happened to start to understand something about me recently, and I've witnessed an explosion of interesting videos from all different kinds of topics. Some of these vids have proved enduring, crucial to my self-development.

I am lucky that on Twitter I get at least 2 feeds to select from, 'Follows' and the falsely endearing 'For You' page, which, as an algo, has shown itself to be engagement-greedy, rather than exclusively holding the intention to be in my best interest.

I love that YouTube learned that I want to see 0 and low-view videos, there's an endearing authenticity to them. 

Yes, YouTube, I will watch them and leave early comments for the next viewers. But why did I have to get lucky/couldn't just manually opt in?
https://t.co/NQ0DbPifej

In an era where curation of your content feed is so important, why do I have to get lucky for the algo to 'get' something fundamental abt me & give me the most transformative content? why can't I fine tune the knobs of MY algorithm, like a modular synth?

@Malcolm_Ocean Open-source software is composed of algorithmic code that others can utilize to achieve desired ends. This is a piece of FOSS psychotech which a group can employ to achieve meta-trust. The aspect of this being 'open', and thus independently verifiable by participants, is key.

@Malcolm_Ocean I think this applies to a lot of other important documents that affect groups as well. 

State/national constitutions are, in some sense, open-source (known). Though none of them are democratically remixable, ie hosted on GitHub and subject to Pull Requests by anyone interested.

@Malcolm_Ocean Constitutions ought not be very mutable, but new states always do pop up (ie network states, and the like) , and it would be wonderful for the best ideas to be easily accessible remixable.

@Malcolm_Ocean With FOSS Constitutions, it can become possible to democratically curate the best remixes to constitutions, with inspired users creating branches to cover edge cases, and really an extremely thoroughly thought-out document with excellent documentation of tests for the document.

"X good, Y bad" is bad

you must see with nuance, beyond the black and white.

but as that first step towards accepting the downsides of Y, it's soo helpful.

"X good, Y bad" is good, sometimes. 

it's not the final resting place, the full truth.

it's a stepping stone to "sometimes X is good and sometimes Y is good--like everything, it depends on the context"

I prefer to have conversations past the overton window, but unfortunately I can end up in conversations where saying

"Y is good sometimes" 

gets twisted. Someone who has the worldview "X is good, Y is bad" could think I'm saying "X bad, Y good", and have a big problem with that

I want to put out a sonar blast that people who interact with me online can fairly assume I'm extremely open-minded, curious about your opinion, not offended by differing beliefs, and I don't take anything I believe so seriously that I can't change my mind in spite of evidence.

I want to put out a sonar blast also that I won't ad-hominem you, or attack you for having different opinions than me. These are the table stakes I see for having a discussion, and they go both ways. 

these are core to who i am, and how i show up online

This is the sign I can point to and say "these are the table stakes for us having an actual conversation, where there is both talking and listening going on"

👆

open-mindedness is currency! 

i assume open-mindedness, and you can expect it from me! that's the whole point of any discussion i have with people ever!

if you are actually looking for the truth, with sincerity and earnestness, it's easy to accept others who differ in opinion from you.

"i do understand why you think that way, but..."

"I don't understand why you think that. Could you explain?"

Universal Basic Intelligence

everyone will benefit from access to superintelligent agents acting on their behalf. not uniformly, but there will be a floor of benefit. a Universal Basic Intelligence that mostly all ppl will have access to, and benefit greatly for it.

A personalized confidant, therapist, job hunter helper, and buddy all in one. Someone to ping-pong your ideas off of, who sees the holes in your thinking and assists you with making logical connections. Someone who knows you and gives you personalized emotional help.

There will be those who benefit disproportionately-- who know how to utilize agents to game the system, riding the frontier of the technology, harnessing it to radically improve their career to the detriment of others.  unfortunately, capital is scarce.

but well-being is not.

There is a floor of benefit to people, when all people have access to Universal Basic Intelligence. humans are beautifully flawed creatures, and we each need help. We make logical fallacies, we harbor emotions that trap us in perpetual worlds of hurt. Unprocessed trauma.

It takes a village of compassionately supportive relationship to raise a child, but even a village isn't enough. The deep internal loneliness of uncaught logical errors and knotted emotional tangles. We need intelligent feedback to soothe our tormented minds and to think better.

The only problem looming on the horizon is the growing investment in AI being a growing promise to extract value out of each and every person who utilizes this intelligence. I pray that people's minds aren't exploited, intelligence compromised like a VR metaverse to feed them ads

I pray that people have access to free open online platforms. That the paid platforms are cheap. That there are integrations accessible everywhere they would be needed. That interfaces are intuitive. That people leave grateful to have these cognitive and emotional prosthetics.

Humans deserve to be smarter. Humans deserve to have higher agency to solve their problems and the problems of their contemporary world. Solving the global coordination problem around climate change is one such example.

Together, with great agency and no scarcity of capital, we can turn our focus to the great problems of our time. Our attention naturally shifting to those spots in our world where things aren't as they should be, and smoothing them out with concerted directed effort.

In the year 30,024 we were someone's cool aliens, growing as a civilization enough to seed intelligent life in an adjacent solar system, watching them grow enough to wonder about the world, and then fucking with them by playing pranks and creating little UFO anomalies.

And when we saw them struggling with paying taxes and feeling lonely, we sent down little tablets that altered their timelines, allowing them to progress further, faster, happier, and with less strife than we did. We were the cool uncles. They got to learn from our mistakes.

Really it was this video that caused this whole thing "What Factorio Is Like For Someone Who Doesn't Play Factorio". I thought i was the "Someone Who Doesn't Play", and my annoyance would repel me from the game.

https://t.co/1lbna7R1ln

Now, I haven't ascertained where Factorio falls between "brainrot" and "what Minecraft could've been". I think part of what Nick and others point to is that Factorio encourages you to "optimize for optimization's sake", because it is kinda fun.

But something gets lost when you disconnect optimization from its ends. You lose the signal to stop optimizing. Sorta the same as Incremental games, which bootstrap the stimulus of 'number go up', only more active, chasing a sense of having getting somthng 'perfectly optimized'.
Factorio also allows you to hone certain systems building, organizational, and management skills. It's not entirely divorced from having ends.

But video games are great for the same reason they suck, which is they are sandbox environments isolated from the emotions of everyday living. They aren't related to the challenges u encounter in ur life, & don't help you overcome them. If they were, that would defeat their fun.

I can't say I fully understand the knowledge expressed by Nick's initial tweet, but it has sat with me since I first saw ~2 days ago. I imagine the sense was that TMI has you grinding w/o purpose, divorced from its promised fruit. I'm not that far into SoE, but mb its different.

+ oh yeah and it's scary to see that many people have a relationship to factorio that is one of powerful addiciton like, "it's unsafe for me to be around this game bc then i can't have the future that I want". like the game actively robs them of Life Force Energy. not good

it's tricky because there is a nice aspect of the game. you can be mindful and appreciate it. however, there is also a mindless (or, intelligenced, but decidedly un-mindful) way you can relate to the game. this has a high ceiling.

probably a large problem for gifted children who minmaxed in intelligence, were highly praised early on for this, but had limited life success outside of this narrow domain. and now can use the game to fulfill their deepest desires of competency,
and imagine this getting their needs met, as was promised to them in the compact they made early on of "if you get really smart and be successful i promise you will get all of the things you need."
many such cases (of directionless smarticle particles produced by this environment)
a lot of my friends here on this platform are fellow wayward smarticle particles. how do you do, fellow particles?

anyways, i think the warning not to get lost in the process, but to focus on reason, meaning, and purpose is important.

raison d'etre creates the stop condition for capitalism's endless production, for playing videogames, or for being a productivity hack addict. lmaooo 🔁❌

circling back, modular synthesizers are an obviously pro-social + pro-ends way to harness the means that are enjoying factorio-type stuff.

it's not repetitive and numbing because it's inherently qualitative; your listening ear is always in-the-loop

now is a great time to propose the question: how do we replace traditional profit-based insurance systems with a network-based "risk-sharing" approach?

ie, the entire world is a group of friends pooling their money together to make sure that everyone gets access to the care they need, including our friends w/ strong needs such as chronic health problems.

fairness to the network is important here, and there are challenges systematizing moral/ethical decisions, but I think it can be done. if you redistribute the profit from healthcare companies, there is way way more than enough funds to go around to cover cheap healthcare for all.

the idea of for-profit healthcare is a problem of misaligned incentives

why is every node in the healthcare system incentivized to keep you keep you sick (ie paying for recurring prescriptions and treatment)?

the biggest barrier I see between where we are rn and a universal general-purpose AI is the ability of an LLM system to prompt engineer itself.

this requires the system to have a degree of meta-cognition: the ability to predict how it might respond in a given scenario, evaluate the effectiveness of that response *among all possible outcomes*, and then choose a better response.

I think that the *among all possible outcomes* part is the hardest. it requires a certain un-anchoredness that humans in particular are really good at. eg successful people r anchored less in short-term failures and more in the long-term vision of what they see self as capable of

The tension between authenticity and ‘game’ (romantic or social more broadly)

Having cptsd teaches you that min-maxing for authenticity (bc it’s all you have) isn’t good enough. You have to have good game because we are all each others’ keys; saying “im a lock i need a key” = 💔

This was a massive insight for me

“just be yourself” attracted dangerous people who desired to exploit my weakness, as well as kind people who pitied me. Sometimes you need to put in work to attract the right people. 

Takeaway: The social fluff is extremely necessary!‼️

Autism also teaches you this, unfortunately in the most indecipherable way possible. 

And yet I’ve seen autistic people who replaced authentic confusion with robotically good game => social butterflies with ease. There is always a way. You define the parameters of your reality.

Balaji talks about this, that many or most movements end up becoming the opposite of what their initial intentions were. 

“You either die the hero, or live long enough to see yourself become the villain”-type beat.

I envision schooling where kids are encouraged to move, talk and play, they are following their curiosity, and most time is spent outdoors https://t.co/eMCrqYbFxa
American schooling is unnatural: stuck indoors, forcing dynamic children to be quiet & still, making them hone their fact memorization instead of following their curiosity
Kids have an innate brilliance for asking the right questions, tearing the world apart, and imagining it differently.

This is an insane skill we can leverage to create real change in the world, and raise a generation of innovators. On the whole we’ve seen kids’ imaginations as Not Very Valuable and squandered or actively blocked in their development.
“Do as you’re told”-maxxing is a good way to raise an NPC, not a human being.

I envision a school system where children feel empowered to create & envision. Where they learn things at different rates. Where their interests guide their intellectual development and they constantly feel empowered by the new concepts they’re learning.
